honesty_notes: n = 4. Per-astronaut effects are reported; we do NOT compute group-level p-values. Within-cohort rankings are descriptive, not inferential.

Risk-profile axes are scored against each astronaut's own preflight baseline AND against population-reference ranges where the source CSV embeds them (CMP, CBC). Multiplex cytokine panels lack embedded ranges, so population_z is sparse for those analytes — this is documented per axis in the Methods expander.

Strict 4-of-4 concordance plus |log2FC| ≥ 1 found no concordant signal in serum cytokines (OSD-575), urine inflammation (OSD-656), stool (OSD-630), CBC (OSD-569), snATAC-seq, or V(D)J. Absence here likely reflects inter-individual variability rather than absence of biological effect.
OSD-571 is supplied as a pre-aggregated DE table (one logFC per analyte). The Mitochondrial axis is therefore cohort-level only and shown with the same trajectory across all four crew.

panels.label: Individualized Risk Profile
panels.intro_md: **Track 2 deliverable.** Four axes — immune regulation, inflammation & oxidative stress, DNA damage response, mitochondrial function — each scored per astronaut, per timepoint, against own preflight baseline and against published population reference ranges where available. Trajectory charts show the full pre/in-flight/post arc; in-flight slots are masked when the underlying assay required ground-only collection.

views.title: Four-axis summary across all four crew

panels.label: AI · grounded in the data
panels.intro_md: Two AI features grounded in the precomputed risk-profile JSON. The model never sees raw OSDR data — only `data/dashboard_data.json`, the same file the rest of the dashboard reads. Every numeric claim in AI output is verified against that JSON; any unverified number is flagged. See [`AI_PLAN.md`](../AI_PLAN.md) for the design rationale.

panels.label: Upstream Causes
panels.intro_md: Trace observed perturbations back toward likely root causes — proteins or molecules that microgravity, radiation, or fluid shifts most directly act on. Each cascade below is a literature-defined chain (stressor → root-cause node → intermediates → observable molecules) scored against the actual analysis output. **Hypothesis-generating, not causal proof** — n=4 cannot prove a chain; we are flagging that the observed pattern is consistent with a known upstream cause, with the cited literature doing the causal lifting.

views.title: Network view: stressors → root causes → match

panels.label: Capsule → Barrier → Systemic Flow
panels.intro_md: Per-astronaut signature visual: capsule-environment microbiome → host-site colonization → skin-barrier response → systemic cytokine response. Edge color encodes evidence strength. The temporal-precedence edges (capsule → host) are directional; correlation-only edges are reported as undirected associations.

panels.label: Molecular Perturbations · Microbiome
panels.intro_md: The strongest concordant signal in the Inspiration-4 data lives in the microbiome. Skin and oral swabs (OSD-572) shifted in the same direction across all four crew at multiple body sites. Capsule-surface taxa (OSD-573) detected during flight overlap meaningfully with crew shifts. Skin barrier transcriptomics (OSD-574) shows a downstream pooled response.

views.title: Skin barrier transcriptomics (post vs pre, pooled)

panels.label: Molecular Perturbations · Systemic
panels.intro_md: Where the per-subject filter found cohort-concordant signal in blood, and where pooled DE captured systemic changes that single-subject testing could not.

panels.label: Data Sources & Provenance
panels.intro_md: Every dataset and external reference the dashboard pulls from. Internal: nine OSDR datasets ingested by `analysis/`. External: literature-derived population reference values and prior-cohort overlays. Calibrations are clearly flagged.

views.title: What's read where, and from whom
